##### SERVICES

> ANTI WRINKLE INJECTIONS

When looking to reduce the appearance of facial wrinkles, anti-wrinkle injections can produce effective results. Infused with purified proteins, they work by blocking nerve signals in the muscles, when injected. The injections contain a naturally occurring neurotoxin, botulinum toxin type A, which helps stop the muscle from over contracting and wrinkling the skin.

> ADVANCED BOTOX TREATMENTS

Botulinum Toxin or Dermal Fillers can be used to relax muscles and tissue, or mask the underlying problem, in even more places in the right hands and can give incredible lasting results.

> PLASMA SKIN TIGHTENING

Plasma skin tightening treatments are pioneering treatment technology that provides a non-surgical solution for the tightening and lifting of loose or wrinkled skin.

> B12 INJECTIONS

Injections are usually given as hydroxocobalamin or cyanocobalamin. These are very effective at raising blood levels of B12 and preventing/reversing a deficiency.

> DERMAL FILLER

Dermal fillers work by boosting the skin’s supply of hyaluronic acid, dermal fillers revitalise and add volume to the skin, instantly diminishing the appearance of lines and wrinkles, and giving a natural softer look.

> MICRO NEEDLING

Microneedling is a dermaroller procedure that uses tiny needles to prick the skin. The purpose of treatment is to generate new collagen and skin tissue for smoother, firmer, more toned skin.

> SEMI PERMANENT MAKEUP

Semi-permanent makeup can be used to enhance features such as lips, eyes and brows.

It is becoming increasingly popular in the northwest.

> INJECTABLE SKIN BOOSTERS

Injectable skin boosters improve the hydration and density of the skin, diminish fine lines and give the complexion a youthful glow. The procedure is safe, effective and non-permanent.

> FAT DISSOLVE INJECTIONS  AND RADIO FREQUENCY SKIN TIGHTENING TREATMENT

Fat dissolving injection that works by liquidating fat cells and destroying them permanently. The broken-down cells are then eliminated naturally through the lymphatic system.
